content.full_text:
THE LAW
OFFICES OF
ROBERT
J LOGAN

152 NORTH THIRD STREET
SUITE 201 • SAN JOSE, CA 95112
TELEPHONE • 408.287.2156
FACSIMILE • 408.280.1749

December 3, 1992

Mr. John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL
PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

RE: FILE NO. I-92-502, RESPONSE TO YOUR LETTER OF
NOVEMBER 23, 1992

Dear Mr. Wallace:

Thank you for the response to our request for the informal assistance letter
which I received yesterday. Since our last correspondence, the individual who asked
me to seek your advice lost his office as a City Councilmember. I have no further
directions to pursue the request further.

I appreciate your looking at the matter. Should you desire any further
information please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly, yours,

Robert J. Logan, Esq.

RJL:jjb
cc: Ed Lang

THE LAW
OFFICES OF
ROBERT
J. LOGAN

SEP 2 8 1992

152 NORTH THIRD STREET
SUITE 201 • SAN JOSE, CA 95112
TELEPHONE • 408.287.2156
FACSIMILE • 408.280.1749

September 24, 1992

Mr. John W. Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion/Second Unit

Dear John:

I am writing seeking another opinion from the Commission. I understand that
you may not be the person answering my request, but thought you might be able to
direct it to the appropriate person.

The facts underlying this request are as follows. A councilmember owns a
second unit on his property. According to the information provided to me, the unit
was constructed pursuant to a building permit issued on April 13, 1984. A notice of
completion was filed on January 6, 1986. The second unit was originally occupied
by his mother-in-law, but is recently occupied by a person who shares in the expenses
and serves as a caretaker in the absence of the councilmember.

The city council was recently presented with a second-unit ordinance
amendment. This amendment eliminates age restrictions and other restrictions
related to second units. Prior to the proposed amendment, second units could only
be used by individuals who were 62 years or older, or individuals who were physically
handicapped. These restrictions would be removed by the amendment. The
amendment also limits the number of new second units to 10 per year and addresses
the number of units that could be legalized during any one-year period.

At the time of introduction, the councilmember discussed the ordinance and
participated in voting in the introduction. The ordinance has not be returned to the
council for final adoption. Following the council discussions, the councilmember
approached me concerning the potential problem regarding his second unit. I advised
him to obtain an opinion from the FPPC before he participated any further on this
ordinance.

The councilmember provided me with the information reflected above. I have
not confirmed any of the dates or information. He is requesting an opinion from the
Commission as to whether it is appropriate for him to vote on this ordinance as he
did not think it would have a material affect on his ownership of his unit.

1


The introduced ordinance, by eliminating the restrictions, opens up the
possibility of rentals to third parties to a much larger population than heretofore
existed. It also, as noted above, allows units which might have a questionable legal
status to undergo a process to legalize those units.

These are the only facts presently available, but I believe they are sufficient
in order for you to review and give us a preliminary determination. If you need
additional information, please do not hesitate to call. In the meantime, the
councilmember will not participate in any fashion with respect to the ordinance.

Thank you in advance for your cooperation.

Sincerely yours,

Robert J. Logan

kl
cc: Lang

2


THE LAW
OFFICES OF
ROBERT
J LOGAN
152 NORTH THIRD STREET
SUITE 201 • SAN JOSE, CA 95112
TELEPHONE • 408.287.2156
FACSIMILE • 408.280.1749

SENT VIA FAX (916) 327-2026 HARD COPY TO FOLLOW

September 24, 1992

Mr. John W. Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion

Dear Mr. Wallace:

In response to your request for information regarding the population within the
1500 radius of the Sky Park project, we submit the following. Based on the 1990
federal census, we have determined there are 1,721 individuals living within the 1500
foot radius out of a total population of 8,615, i.e., 19.97%. The total population of
Scotts Valley as of January 1992, by the City's unofficial count, was 9,118. Even
assuming the number of residents living within the 1500 radius remained constant,
that still represents 18.87% of the population of the City of Scotts Valley.

Given this new information, could you please confirm that the effect on the
public generally would be considered the same as the effect on the planning director.
We would appreciate your prompt reply. If you have any other questions, please do
not hesitate to call.

Sincerely, yours,

Robert J. Logan
City Attorney

RJL/kl
cc: Hanna
